title: Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church
tagline: Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Thomas Orthodox Church is an ancient church located at Mulanthuruthy in Ernakulam district in Kerala, India. It is part of the Kochi Diocese of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church. This church was founded and established in the 11th century A.D. The church was consecrated on 3rd Karkkidakkam (July), in between 1100 and 1125 A.D. The church was modified in the 16th century A.D. The carvings on the main door of the church, made of granite, on which is inscribed in Syrian script, read as ʹʹthe door of the Mar Thoman Church was re-erected on 9th Thulam 1575 A.D.ʹʹ.

topPlaces.name: Cherukavu
topPlaces.description: Cherukavu is a census town in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala. Cherukav panchayath shares its borders with Ramanattukara municipality, Pulikkal panchayath, Vazhayur panchayath pallikkal panchayat and Chelembra panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Aikkarappadi
topPlaces.description: Aikkarappadi (also known as Ayikkarappadi) is a town in the Malappuram district in Kerala, India. Agriculture and education are its main industries.

topPlaces.name: Pallikkal Bazar
topPlaces.description: Pallikkal (Pallikkal Bazar) is a village and gram panchayat in Kondotty Taluk, Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Calicut International Airport at Karipur is near Pallikkal.

topPlaces.name: Idimuzhikkal
topPlaces.description: Idimuzhikkal or Chelembra is a town in Malappuram district, Kerala, India. Chelembra is the Panchayath (One of the elementary governmental ruling systems in India) and Idimuzhikal is the capital of this Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Vazhayur
topPlaces.description: Vazhayur is a census town in Malappuram in the state of Kerala, India. The Vazhayur Census Town has a population of 36,909, of which 18,163 are males and 18,746 are females, as per the report released by Census India 2011.

topPlaces.name: Perinthalmanna taluk
topPlaces.description: Perinthalmanna Taluk, formerly known as Walluvanad Taluk, comes under Perinthalmanna revenue division in Malappuram district of Kerala, India. Its headquarters is the town of Perinthalmanna.

topPlaces.name: Tirur Taluk
topPlaces.description: Tirur Taluk comes under Tirur revenue division in Malappuram district in the Indian state of Kerala. Its headquarters is the town of Tirur.

topPlaces.name: Kondotty taluk
topPlaces.description: Kondotty Taluk, otherwise called Western Eranad, in Malappuram district of Kerala, India. Its headquarters is the town of Kondotty.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church?
faq.a: Stays in Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mulanthuruthy Marthoman Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cherukavu, Aikkarappadi, Pallikkal Bazar, Idimuzhikkal, Vazhayur.
